name: Peñagalera Ascent Trail (PR-TE 151)
name_original: Ascensión a Peña Galera – circular desde Beceite (PR-TE 151)
description: This difficult 11.8-mile circular hike from Beceite to Peña Galera offers challenging terrain and panoramic views of the Els Ports massif.
region_intro: Hiking around Beceite offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by rugged mountains, verdant forests, and crystal-clear rivers. The region features impressive river gorges with spectacular limestone formations, such as those found along the Matarraña River. Hikers can explore mountainous areas like the Els Ports massif, which provides challenging terrain and panoramic views. This varied topography makes Beceite a prime destination for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Beceite The most popular hiking route is Route of Parrissal through the walkways of the Natural Path of Matarraña Algars, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route features wooden walkways spanning clear pools and winding paths through rocky terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñagalera Ascent Trail (PR-TE 151), a difficult 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This challenging circular hike provides extensive views over the Els Ports massif and the Ulldemó River valley. Local hikers also love the Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail from Beceite, a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Beceite is defined by river gorges, limestone formations, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging multi-day treks. The routes in Beceite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Beceite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Beceite?
A: Beceite offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 60 trails to explore. These routes cater to different fitness levels, from easy walks to challenging multi-day treks through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Beceite has 14 easy hiking routes perfect for families and beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974413961/route-of-parrissal-through-the-walkways-of-the-natural-path-of-matarrana-algars" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of Parrissal through the walkways of the Natural Path of Matarraña Algars&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, known for its wooden walkways over clear pools and accessible paths through rocky terrain, making it suitable for all ages.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Beceite?
A: Beceite is rich in natural beauty, featuring impressive river gorges like the Matarraña River gorge with its spectacular limestone formations known as 'Gubies'. You'll also find numerous natural pools, three prominent rivers (Matarraña, Ulldemó, and Pena), and mountainous areas such as the Els Ports massif. The region is characterized by endless forests, unique rock formations, and occasional caves and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or natural pools for swimming?
A: Yes, Beceite is known for its beautiful waterways. The Natural Area of La Pesquera, formed by the Ulldemó River, offers picturesque landscapes with natural pools. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a notable highlight in the area, which can be reached via various trails.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Beceite?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981149235/penagalera-ascent-trail-pr-te-151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñagalera Ascent Trail (PR-TE 151)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.8-mile (19.0 km) circular hike offering extensive views over the Els Ports massif and the Ulldemó River valley. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e953803638/stars-of-the-south-parrissal-de-beceite-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stars of the South: Parrissal de Beceite Trail&lt;/a&gt;, also rated difficult.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Beceite?
A: Yes, many trails in Beceite are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981149235/penagalera-ascent-trail-pr-te-151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñagalera Ascent Trail (PR-TE 151)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable circular option. Additionally, the region offers routes like the Pozas del Río Matarraña Circular Route, which explores natural pools and historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Beceite?
A: Beceite is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring. During warmer months, the natural pools and rivers provide refreshing swimming opportunities, making them particularly appealing.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Beceite?
A: The hiking routes in Beceite are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 220 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, stunning natural landscapes, and the well-maintained paths, especially those along the river gorges.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagalera Summit (1,035 m)&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular panoramic views over the Els Ports massif and the Ulldemó River valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt; themselves are a unique landmark, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is another impressive natural formation.

Q: Is there parking available for popular trailheads, especially for El Parrizal?
A: Yes, for popular areas like El Parrizal de Beceite, parking is typically available. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot, as this area can attract many visitors due to its scenic beauty and accessible routes.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other outdoor activities in Beceite?
A: Beceite is a hub for various outdoor pursuits beyond hiking. You can also enjoy mountain biking, horse riding, cycling the Via Verde of Val del Zafán, and kayaking in the Pantano de Pena. The region's diverse landscape supports a wide range of activities.

Q: Are there any multi-day hiking options in the Beceite region?
A: For serious hikers, El Gran Paseo de los Puertos de Beceite is an 86 km multi-day route, typically completed in 4 days. This immersive trek traverses some of the wildest mountain massifs of the Iberian Peninsula, linking villages across Aragon, Valencia, and Catalonia.
tips: A beautiful and picturesque town typical of the interior of Turolense. It has all the services you need and is an ideal point not only to walk its streets but also to start your routes, whether they are hiking or cycling.

The Toll de Pablet is the starting point of the classic ascent to Penyagalera and must be crossed by ford. If it's been raining a lot, you'll definitely have to take off your boots and pull up your pants.
It should be borne in mind that it is a very frequented area in the summer and there are several car parks distributed at the height of the different hills.
A good place to soak after a magnificent excursion such as climbing the Penyagalera.

Penyagalera is a solitary and unique massif, with rocks and vertical escarpments, but despite this, easily accessible from the Beseit side, and with an extraordinary view of the northern sector of Les Ports and the adjoining plain. On clear days, only this last view pays off the effort. If we go there early in the morning on summer days, the possibility of taking a pleasant swim in the beautiful and wide pools of the Ulldemó river, with transparent water, is a double reward.
The ascent that we could consider normal is the one that goes up from La Pesquera and is signposted as PR-TE 151, we did it downhill. Coming from Arnes, in our case, you have to go up following the Barranc de la Cova, a natural separation between the Penyagalera and the Serrall de les Clapisses, to the Coll de Pelele, from where we will go up a fairly straight path to the ridge where, we will reach the top more gently.
Spectacular viewpoint on the Pesquera path

faqs.question: Where does the Ascension to Peña Galera trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically begins in the urban area of Beceite, often near the asphalted road of the Ulldemó river, also known as 'Las Pesqueras'. Parking is usually available in or around Beceite village, which serves as the main access point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Ascension to Peña Galera – circular from Beceite (PR-TE 151) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the area, given the demanding nature of this route with very stony sections, steep ascents and descents, and loose stones, it's crucial to assess if it's suitable for your dog's fitness and paw resilience. Always keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially mountain goats and griffon vultures, and to respect other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Peña Galera circular trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this trail are generally spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. It's advisable to avoid periods of heavy rainfall, as the Ulldemó river's flow can increase suddenly, making crossings difficult or dangerous. Summer can be very hot, so early morning starts are recommended if hiking during that season.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Puertos de Beceite, specifically for the PR-TE 151 trail?
faqs.answer: Currently, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the PR-TE 151 trail or access the general Puertos de Beceite area. However, always check local regulations before your visit, as rules can change. It's important to respect the natural environment and follow any posted guidelines.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Peña Galera circular route?
faqs.answer: Expect a diverse and demanding mountain terrain. The route features very stony sections, steep ascents and descents, and areas with loose stones. You'll walk through forested areas and cross the Ulldemó river multiple times. Appropriate mountain footwear is essential due to the uneven and challenging surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and viewpoints along the PR-TE 151 trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning scenery, including excellent views of the initial parts of the Els Ports massif. You'll encounter striking geological formations and impressive rock features shaped by erosion. Highlights include the Pablet Pool with its crystal-clear natural pools, and the panoramic vistas from Penyagalera Summit (1,035 m). From the summit, you can see the valleys of the Algars and Ulldemó rivers, nearby towns, and the characteristic reddish rock cliffs of Puertos de Beceite. The Racó de Sant Antoni also offers great views.

faqs.question: What makes the Peña Galera circular trail challenging?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated 'difficult' due to its significant elevation changes and technical sections. It includes a challenging 4 km climb with few breaks and a technical 3 km descent towards Collado de Pelele, which features steep sections and loose stones. The overall distance of nearly 19 km also contributes to its demanding nature.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers about 89% of the 'Subida a Peñagalera' trail and approximately 60% of the 'Estels del Sud' trail. You'll also find it intersecting with parts of 'El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat' and various local paths like 'Camino a Peñagalera' and 'Camino de Les Escales'.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in wildlife. Hikers have a good chance of spotting local fauna such as mountain goats navigating the rocky slopes. Keep an eye on the sky for griffon vultures soaring overhead, which are a common sight in the Els Ports massif.

faqs.question: Are there any historical elements or points of interest besides natural features?
faqs.answer: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, the trail offers glimpses into the region's past. Along the way, you can pass by the ruins of Mas de Blas and an old cobbled threshing floor, which are remnants of past agricultural life in these remote mountain areas.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail frequently interacts with the Ulldemó river, and you'll cross it multiple times. While the river offers natural bathing areas like Pablet Pool, it's not recommended to drink untreated river water. Always carry sufficient water for the entire hike, as there are no reliable potable water sources directly on the trail.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, wear sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, as the trail has very stony sections and loose stones. Dress in layers suitable for mountain weather, and bring rain gear regardless of the forecast. Essential items include plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot's app), sun protection, and a first-aid kit.
